description_long: This object is a solar energy infrastructure installation featuring three distinct solar panel arrays mounted on a large, flat, rectangular platform. The panels are arranged in a staggered, linear configuration, each tilted at an angle to maximize sunlight exposure. The platform appears to be constructed from a light-colored, rigid material, possibly concrete or metal, with raised edges and a slightly textured surface. The solar panels themselves are composed of dark photovoltaic cells set within metallic frames, and are supported by visible mounting brackets. There are no visible electrical inverters or cabling on the surface, suggesting these components may be integrated or concealed. The overall design is robust and utilitarian, suitable for placement on rooftops, parking structures, or other urban environments.
description: A large, flat platform with three tilted solar panel arrays mounted in a staggered linear arrangement, featuring metallic frames and a light-colored base.
description_view_0: Front view showing the three solar panel arrays aligned on the platform, with the panel surfaces and their supporting frames clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the tilt angles of the solar panels and the elongated, flat nature of the supporting platform.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the backs of the solar panels and the full length of the platform, with raised edges visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the staggered arrangement and tilt of the solar panels, as well as the platform's structural details.
attribute_panel_orientation: Panels are tilted at approximately 30-35 degrees, facing a consistent direction, with a fixed orientation.
attribute_mounting_structure_type: roof-mounted or platform-mounted with visible metallic support brackets and a rigid base structure, likely metal or concrete.
attribute_array_configuration: Three solar panel arrays arranged in a staggered, linear row, each panel set apart with visible spacing.
